UserAdminModule — Function Reference

PowerShell Module Viewer
📄

UserAdminModule Function Reference

Select a submodule from the left panel to browse functions, then click a function to view its full help documentation.

Getting Started
How to set up UserAdminModule in your PowerShell profile
Step 1 — Prerequisites
  • PowerShell 5.1 (Windows) or 7+ (cross-platform)
  • Git installed — git-scm.com
  • A local folder for your repos, e.g. C:\GitRepos\
Step 2 — Clone the Repository
Open a terminal and run:
git clone https://github.com/BanterBoy/RDGScripts.git C:\GitRepos\RDGScripts
Step 3 — Check / Create Your Profile
Check whether a profile file already exists:
Test-Path $PROFILE
If it returns False, create it:
New-Item -ItemType File -Path $PROFILE -Force
Step 4 — Configure Your Profile
Add the appropriate dot-source line to your $PROFILE:
PowerShell 7+ (pwsh)
# Add to your $PROFILE
. C:\GitRepos\RDGScripts\SharedPowershellProfle.ps1
Windows PowerShell 5.1
. C:\GitRepos\RDGScripts\SharedWindowsPowershellProfle.ps1
To open your profile in Notepad for editing, run: notepad $PROFILE
Step 5 — Reload & Verify
Reload your profile in the current session:
. $PROFILE
Confirm the module loaded successfully:
Get-Module -Name UserAdminModule* | Select-Object Name, Version
💡 Tip: Once loaded, run Open-ModuleMenuApp (alias: omma) at any time to open this Function Reference in your browser. Use Open-ModuleMenuApp -Regenerate to rebuild it after adding new functions.
